tiddlers.title: $TestFrameworkPlugin
ParametersTests
tiddlers.text:
Live demonstrations of known bugs, issues and gotchas (traps) in [[Parameters]] handling. Each example runs the `foo.*` macros from [[TestParametersPlugin]] so you can see the failure modes with your own eyes. The macros:

* `foo.hello(firstName, lastName)` → `Hello <first> <last>`
* `foo.test(age, boolean, string)` → reports each value **and its type**
* `foo.object({name, age})` → expects one object

## Baseline (works as intended)

* `<<foo.hello John Smith>>`: <<foo.hello John Smith>>
* `<<foo.test 1 true "Car Wash">>`: <<foo.test 1 true "Car Wash">>
* `<<foo.object name:"John Smith" age:22>>`: <<foo.object name:"John Smith" age:22>>
* `<<foo.object {"name":"John Smith", "age":22}>>`: <<foo.object {"name":"John Smith", "age":22}>>

## Trap: invalid JSON falls through to the tokenizer

JSON keys must be double-quoted. One unquoted key (`age:`) and the whole payload is silently tokenized into garbage strings instead:

* `<<foo.object {"name":"John Smith", age:22}>>`: <<foo.object {"name":"John Smith", age:22}>>

The handler received `('{name:John Smith,', 'age:22}')` — note `name` and `age` are `undefined` above. In **commands** the same mistake at least logs a console warning and passes the raw string.

## ✅ Fixed: a colon inside a named-parameter value (v0.23.0)

`getKeyVal` now splits on the **first** colon only, so values may contain colons (URLs work as named parameters):

* `<<foo.object name:Mr:Smith age:1>>`: <<foo.object name:Mr:Smith age:1>>
* `<<foo.object name:"Mr:Smith" age:1>>`: <<foo.object name:"Mr:Smith" age:1>>

`name` correctly arrives as `Mr:Smith` in both cases. JSON still works too — `<<foo.object {"name":"Mr:Smith", "age":1}>>`: <<foo.object {"name":"Mr:Smith", "age":1}>>

Previously this split at *every* colon, so `name` truncated to `Mr` and URLs could not be passed as named parameters.

## Trap: quoting does not prevent type coercion

You cannot force `"1"` or `"true"` to stay strings — the tokenizer strips quotes *before* coercion:

* `<<foo.test "1" "true" "x">>`: <<foo.test "1" "true" "x">>

`age` is a number and `boolean` a boolean despite the quotes. **Workaround**: JSON — `<<foo.test ["1", "true", "x"]>>`: <<foo.test ["1", "true", "x"]>>

## Trap: $`${expr}` eval tokens

A single `${...}` token is `eval`'d as JavaScript — useful (`${1+2}` → `3`) but two traps:

1. **Basic case** - ``<<foo.test ${tw.tiddlers.all.length} true "Tiddlers Length">>` should `foo.test(tw.tiddlers.all.length, true, 1)` (eval expression): <<foo.test ${tw.tiddlers.all.length} true "Tiddlers Length">>
2. **No spaces allowed** — a space splits the token before eval: `<<foo.test ${1 + 2} true x>>`: <<foo.test ${1 + 2} true x>>
   (`age` arrived as the literal string `{1`.)

## Trap: arrays always spread

A JSON array becomes positional arguments — a handler can never receive a bare array unless you wrap it:

* `<<foo.hello ["John", "Smith"]>>`: <<foo.hello ["John", "Smith"]>> (spread → two parameters)
* `<<foo.hello [["John", "Smith"]]>>`: <<foo.hello [["John", "Smith"]]>> (`firstName` is the whole array, `lastName` is `undefined`)

## Trap: `>` cannot appear in macro parameters

The macro regex stops at the first `>`, so `<<m {1>0}>>` or JSON containing `>` breaks the macro match entirely (it renders literally or mis-parses). There is no escape — keep `>` out of macro parameters.

## Command-only traps (see [[Parameters]] → Macros vs Commands)

* **No type coercion**: `#msg:m:1` delivers the string `'1'` — macros would deliver the number `1`. Use JSON.
* **Uppercase silently truncates the event name**: `#msg:t.Xy:1` fires the event `t.` with payload `Xy:1` — no error. Keep event names `lowercase.dotted`.
* **Spaces break `#msg:` links**: a raw space ends a markdown URL — this link is broken and renders literally: [broken](#msg:search.advanced:pck:icons title:add) — encode as `%20` and it works: [works](#msg:search.advanced:pck:icons%20title:add)
* **Named-parameter parse errors degrade silently**: a payload like `a:{boom}` whose value eval throws is passed to the handler as the raw string, with no warning.
* **`$currentTiddler` is textual**: substitution is a string `replaceAll` — a title containing `"` corrupts surrounding JSON payloads.

tiddlers.title: Test Search Result
tiddlers.text:
A test tiddler used by TestSearch to verify that clicking a multi-word
search result opens the correct tiddler (not just the first word).

tiddlers.title: TestLinks
tiddlers.text:
* <<tests.clear>>
* <<tests.queue name:"Close All Tiddlers" msg:ui.close.all find:[data-tiddler-title] expectNone:1>>
* <<tests.queue name:"Open All Tiddlers" msg:ui.open.all find:[data-tiddler-title] expectSome:"Welcome,Help">>
* <<tests.queue comment:"Done" msg:ui.close.all>>

<<tests.run suite:"Links">>

## To Test 

* Link to search results: [😎Shadows](#msg:search:$tag:$Shadow)
* Open all tagged with `Favorite`: [⭐Favorites](#msg:ui.open.all:{"tag":"Favorite"})
* [Favorite](#msg:ui.open.all:tag:Favorite)

tiddlers.title: TestParametersPlugin
TestRendering
tiddlers.text:
# Rendering

## Code-block escaping

Must render literally, no links/macros/inclusions

Inline code, must stay literal: `[[$StyleSheetCore]]` `<<somemacro>>` `{{SomeTiddler}}`

Fenced block, must stay literal:

```
* [[$StyleSheetCore]]
* <<somemacro>>
* {{SomeTiddler}}
* [Show 'Code Tiddler'](#msg:tiddler.show:Code%20Tiddler)
```

And a real link outside code still works: [[$StyleSheetCore]]

tiddlers.title: TestSearch
tiddlers.text:
* <<tests.clear>>
* <<tests.queue name:"Close All Tiddlers" msg:"ui.close-all" find:[data-tiddler-title] expectNone:1>>
* <<tests.queue name:"Search Welcome" type:#search input:welcome find:"#search-results [data-params*=Welcome]">>
* <<tests.queue name:"Search Advanced 1" type:#search input:"$close pck:icons" find:"#search-results div" expect:"$IconClose">>
* <<tests.queue name:"Search Advanced 2" type:#search input:"$all pck:icons" find:"#search-results div" expect:"$IconOpenAll,$IconCloseAll">>
* <<tests.queue comment:"Search result click — multi-word title">>
* <<tests.queue name:"Search: multi-word result appears" type:#search input:"Test Search" find:"#search-results [data-params*='Test Search Result']">>
* <<tests.queue name:"Click: multi-word result opens correct tiddler" click:"#search-results [data-params*='Test Search Result']" find:"#visible-tiddlers [data-tiddler-title='Test Search Result']">>
* <<tests.queue comment:"Done">>

<<tests.run suite:"Search">>
